Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-tooltip repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: with the click trigger enabled (`openOnClick`), clicking an anchor element is supposed to open its tooltip. The library implements this by listening for click events at the document level. Because that delegated listener runs during the event's bubbling phase, if the anchor's own click handler calls `event.stopPropagation()`, the click never bubbles up to the document, the library's delegated listener never runs, and the tooltip fails to open. The correct behavior is that clicking the anchor opens its tooltip even when the anchor stops propagation of the click event. Fix how/when the library registers its delegated click handling so that a propagation-stopping anchor still opens the tooltip. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: no root access is needed. Dependencies are already installed via `npm install -g yarn@1.22.22 && yarn install --ignore-scripts --ignore-engines`. Run tests with Jest, e.g. `node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js`. The event-delegation logic lives under `src/components/Tooltip/`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

describe('tooltip interaction behavior', () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    jest.useFakeTimers()
    jest.clearAllMocks()
  })

  afterEach(() => {
    flushPendingTimers()
    jest.useRealTimers()
  })

  const renderAndFlush = async (ui) => {
    let view
    await act(async () => {
      view = render(ui)
      await Promise.resolve()
    })
    return view
  }

  test('opens on click when click is the configured trigger', async () => {
    await renderAndFlush(
      <>
        <span data-tooltip-id="click-only-test">Hover Me</span>
        <TooltipController
          id="click-only-test"
          content="Click Only Test"
          openEvents={{ click: true }}
          closeEvents={{ click: true }}
        />
      </>,
    )

    const anchor = screen.getByText('Hover Me')

    hoverAnchor(anchor, 100)
    expect(document.getElementById('click-only-test')).not.toBeInTheDocument()

    fireEvent.click(anchor)
    await flushMicrotasks()
    const tooltip = await waitForTooltip('click-only-test')
    expect(tooltip).toHaveTextContent('Click Only Test')
  })

  test('stops showing after scroll and resize global close events', async () => {
    await renderAndFlush(
      <>
        <span data-tooltip-id="global-events-test">Hover Me</span>
        <TooltipController
          id="global-events-test"
          content="Global Events Test"
          globalCloseEvents={{ scroll: true, resize: true }}
        />
      </>,
    )

    const anchor = screen.getByText('Hover Me')

    hoverAnchor(anchor, 1000)
    await waitForTooltip('global-events-test')

    fireEvent.scroll(window)
    advanceTimers(100)
    await flushMicrotasks()
    await waitForTooltipToStopShowing('global-events-test')

    // Allow the tooltip to fully unmount before re-hovering
    advanceTimers(100)
    await flushMicrotasks()

    hoverAnchor(anchor, 1000)
    await flushMicrotasks()
    await waitForTooltip('global-events-test')

    fireEvent.resize(window)
    advanceTimers(100)
    await flushMicrotasks()
    await waitForTooltipToStopShowing('global-events-test')
  })

  test('keeps a clickable tooltip open while the pointer moves into it', async () => {
    await renderAndFlush(
      <>
        <span data-tooltip-id="clickable-test">Hover Me</span>
        <TooltipController
          id="clickable-test"
          content="Clickable Test"
          clickable
          globalCloseEvents={{ scroll: true, resize: true }}
        />
      </>,
    )

    const anchor = screen.getByText('Hover Me')

    hoverAnchor(anchor, 1000)
    const tooltip = await waitForTooltip('clickable-test')

    unhoverAnchor(anchor, 0)
    fireEvent.mouseEnter(tooltip)
    advanceTimers(150) // past clickable's 100ms hide-delay so the close timer fires
    await flushMicrotasks()

    const elem = screen.getByRole('tooltip')
    expect(elem).toBeInTheDocument()

/**
 * Shared document event delegation.
 *
 * Instead of N tooltips each calling document.addEventListener(type, handler),
 * we maintain ONE document listener per event type. When the event fires,
 * we iterate through all registered handlers for that type.
 *
 * This reduces document-level listeners from O(N × eventTypes) to O(eventTypes).
 */

type Handler = (event: Event) => void

const handlersByType = new Map<string, Set<Handler>>()

function getOrCreateSet(eventType: string): Set<Handler> {
  let set = handlersByType.get(eventType)
  if (!set) {
    set = new Set()
    handlersByType.set(eventType, set)
    document.addEventListener(eventType, dispatch)
  }
  return set
}

function dispatch(event: Event): void {
  const handlers = handlersByType.get(event.type)
  if (handlers) {

When the event\\n * fires, we iterate through all registered handlers for that type.\\n *\\n * This reduces document-level listeners from O(N × eventTypes) to O(eventTypes).\\n */\\n\\ntype Handler = (event: Event) => void\\n\\ntype HandlerSets = {\\n  bubble: Set<Handler>\\n  capture: Set<Handler>\\n}\\n\\nconst handlersByType = new Map<string, HandlerSets>()\\n\\nfunction getOrCreateSet(eventType: string, capture: boolean): Set<Handler> {\\n  let sets = handlersByType.get(eventType)\\n  if (!sets) {\\n    sets = { bubble: new Set(), capture: new Set() }\\n    handlersByType.set(eventType, sets)\\n  }\\n\\n  const handlers = capture ? sets.capture : sets.bubble\\n  if (handlers.size === 0) {\\n    document.addEventListener(eventType, capture ? dispatchCapture : dispatch)\\n  }\\n  return handlers\\n}\\n\\nfunction dispatch(event: Event): void {\\n  handlersByType.get(event.type)?.bubble.forEach((handler) => handler(event))\\n}\\n\\nfunction dispatchCapture(event: Event): void {\\n  handlersByType.get(event.type)?.capture.forEach((handler) => handler(event))\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Register a handler for a document-level event type.\\n *\\n * Capture-phase delegation is useful for events such as clicks: an anchor is\\n * allowed to stop propagation in its own handler, but that must not prevent a\\n * tooltip from seeing the click.\\n * Returns an unsubscribe function.\\n */\\nexport function addDelegatedEventListener(\\n  eventType: string,\\n  handler: Handler,\\n  capture = false,\\n): () => void {\\n  const handlers = getOrCreateSet(eventType, capture)\\n  handlers.add(handler)\\n\\n  return () => {\\n    handlers.delete(handler)\\n    if (handlers.size === 0) {\\n      document.removeEventListener(eventType, capture ? dispatchCapture : dispatch)\\n      const sets = handlersByType.get(eventType)\\n      if (sets && sets.bubble.size === 0 && sets.capture.size === 0) {\\n        handlersByType.delete(eventType)\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Reset for testing purposes.\\n */\\nexport function resetEventDelegation(): void {\\n  handlersByType.forEach((sets, eventType) => {\\n    if (sets.bubble.size) document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatch)\\n    if (sets.capture.size) document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatchCapture)\\n  })\\n  handlersByType.clear()\\n}\\n\"}"}]

- fs_edit(path, old, new): edit an existing file
- bash(cmd): run shell commands

<available_tools>
- bash(cmd, cwd?, timeout_seconds?, background?): Run a shell command. Use background=true to keep it running and fetch output later via bash_output.
- bash_output(shell_id, offset?, limit?): Read buffered output from a background shell, with optional offset/limit for incremental polling.
- bash_kill(shell_id): Terminate a background shell process.
- fs_read(path, offset?, limit?): Read a text file and return its content. Supports optional pagination with offset and limit.
- fs_write(path, content): Write content to a text file.
- fs_edit(path, old, new, start?): Edit a text file by replacing old text with new text. You can specify the line number to start searching for the old text.
- skill(name): Load the skill content by name. Return the location and skill content.
- tape_info(): Get information about the current tape, such as number of entries and anchors.
- tape_search(query, limit?, start?, end?, kinds?)
- tape_reset(archive?): Reset the current tape, optionally archiving it.
- tape_handoff(name?, summary?): Add a handoff anchor to the current tape.
- tape_anchors(): List anchors in the current tape.
- web_fetch(url, headers?, timeout?): Fetch(GET) the content of a web page, returning markdown if possible.
- subagent(prompt, model?, session?, allowed_tools?, allowed_skills?)
- help(): Show a help message.
- quit(): Abort the tasks of the current session. DO NOT use it in a normal workflow.
- model(model_id): Switch the model for THIS session. Invoke as the `,model <model_id>` command.

Takes effect on the NEXT turn and persists across restarts. Pass any
``provider:model`` string (for example ``openai:gpt-4o`` or

next turn — run `,model <valid_id>` again to recover.
